title: The violence specialists
description:
Every society depends on violence workers, but what makes young men take a job that risks their lives and harms others?

- by Raúl Zepeda Gil
hook: A chilling look at how societies train and deploy their violence specialists—and why we pretend not to see them.

title: ‘The Bear’ Finale Is All About the Memories
description: The ending of "The Bear" works best with a selective memory, but our specific connection to these characters — and the family they built — matters most of all.
hook: The Bear's finale works because we've built something real with these people—how memory shapes what a family actually is.

title: Making A Splash: Remembering Hockney Onscreen
description: How Jack Hazan’s 1973 intimate portrait of the late David Hockney at work, play, and through emotional turmoil, strangely anticipated how we consume images of celebrities today.
hook: Jack Hazan's 1973 Hockney film accidentally predicted how we'd obsess over celebrities' inner lives and creative chaos online.

title: Going for the Jugular: On the Recording of the Velvet Underground & Nico
description:
In an exclusive extract from his new book, Do What You Fear Most: The History of the Velvet Underground, author and music journalist Richie Unterberger re-assesses the role played by pop artist Andy Warhol in one of the seminal rock albums of the late 1960s

John Ca
hook: Inside the recording studio where Lou Reed captured something feral and uncertain—the Velvet Underground at their most alive.

title: The “Emotional Economy” of Parenting: A Conversation with Nina Bandelj
description:
“I am an observer of how intellectual ideas travel and enter into everyday life, or how they come to be performed by families in ways that give those ideas a reality of their own.”

The post The “Emotional Economy” of Parenting: A Conversation with Nina Bandelj appeared first on Public Books.
hook: How parenting philosophies become performance, and what happens when intellectual ideas collide with the mess of raising actual children.

title: At the Crucible: Snooker’s World Series
description: “Contemporary snooker is defined by an ongoing anomaly: three men in their fifties still pummel younger players.”
hook: Why three men in their fifties still dominate snooker—and what that says about mastery in a sport built on precision and time.

title: Julián Delgado Lopera on “Pretend You’re Dead and I Carry You”
description:
“I am inhabiting many different identities, sometimes multiple identities, constantly. I feel that I'm definitely building on a legacy of Latin American literature and Latino literature in the United States.”

The post Julián Delgado Lopera on “Pretend You’re Dead and I Carry You” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: A Latin American writer on inhabiting many selves at once, building on queer and immigrant legacies invisible to American literary history.
